phaseB.method: generalized eigenproblem H v=lam M v (M=h^3 I, physical normalization lam_phys=lam_euclid/h^3); FIXED-ASYMPTOTIC perturbations (eta=0 on boundary layer); analytic-symmetry deflation ONLY (3 trans+3 rot+1 target-SO(2)); matrix-free FD-of-gradient HVP; LOBPCG-lite.
phaseB.field: 256^3 PRODUCTION minimizer as-is (virial 0.9995, gradnorm 0.13 -- NOT a true critical point)

residual_gradient_alignment_check.reading: the residual-gradient direction has POSITIVE curvature (+574); the converged negative mode (-312, in_core 0.995) is ORTHOGONAL to it -> the negative is NOT the residual-gradient/settling artifact.

phaseB.multi_resolution_re_relaxation: BLOCKED: naive arrested-Newton collapses the coarse hopfion (Derrick shrink) without the production Derrick-rescale machinery; coarse re-relaxed fields (virial 1.0-1.38, gradnorm grew) are invalid. Correct fix = re-relax with fs_hopfion/drive_production rescale at each grid.
phaseB.verdict: UNRESOLVED (with concern). A CONVERGED, LOCALIZED, NEGATIVE Hessian mode (lam_phys=-312) is found at the 256^3 production field and is NOT the residual-gradient direction. BUT the field is not a true critical point (gradnorm 0.13), so a Hessian does not rigorously certify minimizer (in)stability here. Resolving REQUIRES the Hessian at a properly re-relaxed minimizer (gradnorm->0), which needs the production rescale machinery. Physics prior: Q_H=1 FS hopfion is a known stable soliton -> the true minimizer is likely stable, but THIS numerics neither confirms nor refutes it.
phaseB.gate: UNRESOLVED -- NOT PASS (do not treat the Phase-A/C clean mass as established), NOT FAIL_H3_INSTABILITY (field not critical). Blocked on proper re-relaxation.
phaseB.correction: supersedes the earlier PASS-on-FAIL-criterion Phase B (which used inconsistent Euclidean normalization, no fixed boundary, and un-re-relaxed fields). The rigorous form (Charles 2026-07-11) is inconclusive and surfaces a concern.
